chief_complaint: A 76-year-old man presented with persistent pneumothorax and subcutaneous emphysema after left upper lobe bronchoscopic lung volume reduction.
history: A 76-year-old man with severe emphysema underwent bronchoscopic lung volume reduction (BLVR) with endobronchial valves (EBVs). He developed persistent pneumothorax and subcutaneous emphysema post-procedure.
exam: Vital signs: stable. Physical examination: subcutaneous emphysema and persistent pneumothorax.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: chest X-ray showing persistent pneumothorax and subcutaneous emphysema.
correct_diagnosis: Persistent pneumothorax after bronchoscopic lung volume reduction
differentials: Persistent pneumothorax after bronchoscopic lung volume reduction
correct_ranking: Persistent pneumothorax after bronchoscopic lung volume reduction
